allow: Bash(# Let me check how the existing Gimbal project connects - maybe there''s a config file or special setup find /home/ubuntu/Desktop/Gimbal -name ""*.yaml"" -o -name ""*.yml"" -o -name ""*.cfg"" -o -name ""*.ini"" -o -name ""*.conf"")

Bash(# Nothing works. v1, v2, any baud rate - zero bytes from the gimbal. # But the SimpleBGC Java GUI works. Let me check something fundamental: # maybe the SimpleBGC GUI uses a DIFFERENT serial port or adapter. # Check ALL serial/USB devices ls -la /dev/tty*)

Bash(# Start the SimpleBGC GUI under strace, capture serial-related syscalls # We''ll run it for a few seconds, let it auto-connect, then kill it cd /home/ubuntu/Downloads/SimpleBGC_GUI_2_70b0 && timeout 15 strace -f -e trace=openat,ioctl,read,write,close -o /tmp/java_strace.txt java -Dsimplebgc_gui.SimpleBGC_GUIView.Logger.level=0 -Djava.library.path=""lib"" -Dlog4j.configuration=log4j.properties -Dgnu.io.rxtx.NoVersionOutput=true -Djava.library.path=./lib -Dsun.java2d.dpiaware=false -jar SimpleBGC_GUI.jar 2>&1 & JAVA_PID=$! echo ""Java PID: $JAVA_PID"" # Wait for it to start and potentially connect sleep 12 # Kill it gracefully kill $JAVA_PID wait $JAVA_PID echo ""Java GUI stopped"" # Search for serial operations echo ""=== ttyUSB operations ==="" grep ""ttyUSB"" /tmp/java_strace.txt)
Bash(# Check ch341 kernel module info modinfo ch341)
Bash(# Check if brltty udev rule matches our device grep -A5 -B5 ""1a86.*7523\\|7523"" /usr/lib/udev/rules.d/85-brltty.rules)
Bash(# Check the sbgc_cmd tool cat /home/ubuntu/Downloads/SimpleBGC_GUI_2_70b0/sbgc_cmd.bat # Also try picocom if available which picocom || sudo apt-get install -y picocom)
Bash(# Simple echo test to serial port echo -ne ''\\x3e\\x14\\x00\\x14\\x00'' & timeout 3 cat /dev/ttyUSB0)
Bash(# Let''s examine what the nrjavaserial .so exports and links against nm -D /tmp/libNRJavaSerial_ubuntu_0/libNRJavaSerial.so)
